editions.label: A Gāndhārī  Version of the Anattalakkhaṇa-sutta

lemmas.description: Skt. tatra, P tattha, P tatra
Skt. ittham api, P itthaṃ api
Skt. yasmāt, P yamhā, P yasmā
Skt. tasmin, P tamhi, P tasmiṃ
Skt. āttamanas, BHS āptamanas, P attamana

lemmas.translation: hereness, being here in the world

sequences.translation: [8] Thus I heard.
sequences.chaya: [8] evam mayā śrutam

sequences.translation: At one time the Bhagavat (*dwelt in Baraṇas̱i in the Deer Park at Hiṣ̱ivaḏaṇa.)
sequences.chaya: ekam samayam bhagavān vārāṇasyām viharati r̥ṣipatane mr̥gadāve |

sequences.translation: (*There the Bhagavat) [9] addressed the five monks:
sequences.chaya: tatra khalu bhagavān [9] pañcakān bhikṣūn āmantrayasi (BHS) |

sequences.translation: “(*Form), monks, is not-self.
sequences.chaya: rūpam bhikṣavaḥ anātmā |

sequences.translation: (*If, monks, this form were self), [10] this form would not lead to affliction [and] suffering, and it would be possible to obtain (*with regard to form) ‘[Let] form [11] be (*thus); [let] form be not thus.’
sequences.chaya: rūpam idam bhikṣavaḥ ātmā abhaviṣyat [10] na idam rūpam ābādhāya duḥkhāya saṃvarteta labhyeta ca rūpe evam eva [11] rūpam bhavati (P hotu, Skt. bhavatu) evam rūpam mā bhūt (P ahosi) |

sequences.translation: But since this form (*is not-self, therefore) [12] form leads to affliction [and] suffering and one does not obtain (*with regard to form ‘[Let] form) [13] be (*thus); let form be not thus.’”
sequences.chaya: yasmāt idam rūpam anātmā tasmāt [12] rūpam ābādhāya duḥkhāya saṃvartate no ca labhate rūpe evam eva rūpam [13] bhavati evam rūpam mā bhūt (P ahosi) |

sequences.translation: [14] So also feeling, perception, volitional formations.
sequences.chaya: [14] ittham api vedanā saṃjñā saṃskārāḥ |

sequences.translation: “Consciousness, monks, is not-self.
sequences.chaya: vijñānam bhikṣavaḥ anātmā |

sequences.translation: (*If, monks, this consciousness) [15] were self, this consciousness would not lead to affliction [and] suffering, (*and it would be possible to obtain with regard to consciousness) [16] ‘[Let] consciousness be thus; [let] consciousness be not thus.’
sequences.chaya: vijñānam idam bhikṣavaḥ [15] ātmā abhaviṣyat na idam vijñānam ābādhāya duḥkhāya saṃvarteta labhyeta ca vijñāne [16] evam eva vijñānam bhavati evam eva vijñāne mā bhūt (P ahosi) |

sequences.translation: (*But) since (*this consciousness is not-self), [17] therefore consciousness leads to affliction [and] suffering and one does not obtain with regard to consciousness [18] ‘[Let] consciousness be (*thus); [let] consciousness be not thus.’
sequences.chaya: yasmāt idam vijñānam anātmā [17] tasmāt vijñānam ābādhāya duḥkhāya saṃvartate no ca labhate vijñāne evam eva [18] vijñānam bhavati evam eva vijñānam mā bhūt (P ahosi) ||

sequences.translation: “What do you think, is form permanent or (*impermanent)?”  [19]
sequences.chaya: tad kim manyatha rūpam nityam vā anityam [19] vā |

sequences.translation: “Impermanent, venerable sir.”
sequences.chaya: anityam bhadanta |

sequences.translation: “What is impermanent, is [that] suffering or happiness?”
sequences.chaya: yad anityam duḥkham vā sukham vā |

sequences.translation: “This is suffering, venerable sir.”
sequences.chaya: duḥkham idam bhadanta |

sequences.translation: “Again, what is (*impermanent), [20] suffering, [and] subject to change, is it suitable to regard [this thus] ‘This is mine; I am this; [21] this is my self?’”
sequences.chaya: yad punar anityam [20] duḥkham vipariṇāmadharmam kalyam nu samanupaśyitum (BHS) eṣaḥ mama eṣaḥ aham [uncertain] asmi [21] etad me ātmā iti |

sequences.translation: “This is not [suitable], venerable sir.”
sequences.chaya: na u idam bhadanta |

sequences.translation: “What do you think, is feeling permanent or impermanent?”
sequences.chaya: tad kim manyatha vedanā nityā vā anityā vā |

sequences.translation: “Surely impermanent, [22] venerable sir.”
sequences.chaya: anityā [22] hi bhadanta |

sequences.translation: “What is impermanent, is that suffering or happiness?”
sequences.chaya: yad anityam duḥkham vā tad sukham vā |

sequences.translation: “This is suffering, venerable sir.”
sequences.chaya: duḥkham idam bhadanta |

sequences.translation: “Again, what is impermanent, suffering, [23] [and] subject to change, is it suitable to regard this [thus] ‘This is mine; I am this; this is my self?’” [24]
sequences.chaya: yad punar anityam duḥkham vipariṇāma[23]dharmam kalyam nu etad samanupaśyitum (BHS) eṣaḥ mama eṣaḥ aham [uncertain] asmi etad me ātmā [24] iti |

sequences.translation: “Surely not, venerable sir.”
sequences.chaya: no hi bhadanta |

sequences.translation: So also perception, volitional formations, consciousness.
sequences.chaya: ittham api saṃjñā saṃskārāḥ vijñānam ||

sequences.translation: “(*Seeing thus, the well-taught), [25] noble disciple is disgusted with forms, disgusted with feeling, disgusted with perception, disgusted with (*volitional formations), [26] disgusted with consciousness; being disgusted, he is dispassionate; on account of dispassion, [his mind] is liberated; being liberated, [27] there is the knowledge ‘[my mind] is liberated.’
sequences.chaya: evam paśyan śrutavān [25] āryaśrāvakaḥ rūpeṣu nirvidyate vedanāyām nirvidyate saṃjñāyām nirvidyate saṃskāre[26]ṣu nirvidyate vijñāne nirvidyate nirviṇṇaḥ virajyate virāgāt vimucyate vimukte [27] vimuktam evam jñānam bhavati |

sequences.translation: He understands ‘[re]birth is destroyed; the holy life has been lived; what is to be done has been done by me; [28] there is no further state than this.’”
sequences.chaya: kṣīṇā jāti uṣitam brahmacaryam kr̥tam mayā karaṇīyam [28] na aparam *ihatvam iti prajānāti ||

sequences.translation: And while this exposition was being spoken, [29] the minds of the five monks were liberated from the taints through not clinging.
sequences.chaya: asmin ca vyākaraṇe bhāṣyamāne [29] pañcakānām bhikṣūṇām anupādāya āsravebhyaḥ cittāni vimuktāni |

sequences.translation: [30] The Bhagavat (*said) this.
sequences.chaya: idam avocat [30] bhagavān |

sequences.translation: Pleased, the five monks (*delighted at the speech of the Bhagavat).
sequences.chaya: āttamanasaḥ pañcakāḥ bhikṣavaḥ ||
